package_name: 镜头焦段调节 UI 同步修正
device_feedback: Lens zoom runtime changes during horizontal drag.
Lens ruler UI did not visually follow the zoom change.
Current value badge, center pointer, and tick labels overlapped.

root_cause.summary: The previous lens ruler mapped drag position directly to zoom writes but did not maintain pending zoom, selected tick, or moving tick offset state.
details: Current value depended primarily on runtime write-back, so it could stay visually stale while runtime was changing.
Tick layout was static progress-based, so the ruler appeared not to move during drag.
Selected tick did not snap under a fixed center pointer.
Top value text, center badge, pointer, and tick labels were too dense vertically.

tick_mapping.selected_index: nearest tick to pending/runtime display zoom

pinch_zoom_sync.strategy: When no lens pending zoom exists, the overlay reads cameraRuntime.lensZoomDialValue. Continuous pinch zoom values are inserted into the tick list for display and nearest-index alignment.

xcodebuild.command: xcodebuild -project /Users/sungning/Projects/SellerCamera/SellerCamera.xcodeproj -scheme SellerCamera -configuration Debug -destination "generic/platform=iOS Simulator" CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build

device_run.reason: No physical device execution was performed in this package.

known_risks: Lens ruler UI sync still needs real-device visual confirmation because the original issue was observed on device.
Extreme device zoom ranges may require an additional label-density pass after screenshot review.
The ruler remains discrete; continuous pinch zoom is displayed but not forced into runtime tick values.
